description: Replace hard-DELETE in archive with soft-archive (archived_at column). Add --include-archived flag to list/history. Change init --force to archive before reimport.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Approach before code. Anticipate edge cases. Consider 2-3 approaches, pick the best

2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — If a more sophisticated solution costs ~1 day now but saves ~2+ weeks post-launch, take that trade-off (1:10 ratio or better). We are pre-launch; foundations compound enormously
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable code, wired in according to the plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Self-critique after code. Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Quality dimensions explicit. Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — read qualityDimensions on every task; know what 'good' looks like before coding
Approach before code — consider 2-3 approaches with tradeoffs, pick the best, then implement
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test; boundaries are where bugs hide
Self-critique after code — review your own implementation for correctness, style, and performance before moving on
Ship solid working code with tests to prove it
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies archived_at column exists on all 4 tables after migration
Test verifies archived_at defaults to NULL on inserted rows
Test verifies all 4 indexes exist in sqlite_master
Test verifies schema_version is 14 after migration
Test verifies down migration removes columns and reverts to v13
Tests compile (may use #[ignore] if migration file doesn't exist yet)
userStories.description: Write tests for the v14 migration that adds archived_at columns and indexes to tasks, runs, run_tasks, and key_decisions tables. Tests should verify column existence, NULL defaults, index creation, and down migration. Follow the pattern established by v12 and v13 migration tests.
edgeCases: Down migration must remove columns even if they contain data
Indexes must be named consistently: idx_{table}_archived_at

failureModes.cause: ALTER TABLE fails on one table
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Transaction rolls back, no partial migration

failureModes.cause: Index name conflicts with existing index
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Migration fails cleanly with error message

invariants: Schema version must be 14 after up migration
Schema version must be 13 after down migration
All 4 tables must have archived_at column after migration
userStories.notes: Follow the v12/v13 test patterns in src/db/migrations/v12.rs and v13.rs. Use setup_migrated_db() helper.

correctness: Each column must be verified independently — don't assume one ALTER TABLE success means all succeeded
style: Follow existing migration test patterns exactly

userStories.title: Initial tests for migration v14

acceptanceCriteria: Test: archive PA sets archived_at on PA tasks, PB tasks untouched
Test: archive PA sets archived_at on PA run_tasks, PB run_tasks untouched
Test: shared run (PA + PB run_tasks) stays non-archived when only PA archived
Test: orphaned run (only PA run_tasks) gets archived when PA archived
Test: key_decisions for PA get archived_at set
Test: task_relationships and task_files are hard-deleted (not soft-archived)
Test: archived tasks invisible to status count queries
Test: counter reset works correctly with archived tasks present
Known-bad discriminator: raw query WITHOUT archived_at IS NULL filter still sees archived rows
Tests compile (may reference functions that don't exist yet, use #[ignore])
userStories.description: Write tests for the archive_prd_data function (soft-archive instead of hard-delete) and for query filters that hide archived records. Tests should cover: PA tasks archived while PB untouched, shared runs preserved, key_decisions archived, archived tasks invisible to status queries. Follow existing test patterns in archive.rs test module.
edgeCases: Shared run across PRDs: run-shared has run_tasks for PA-001 and PB-001; archiving PA must NOT archive the run
No files on disk but DB records exist: archive must still set archived_at (learning #565)
Counter reset with archived tasks: iteration_counter reset must use WHERE archived_at IS NULL
Re-archiving idempotency: archiving already-archived records must preserve original archived_at timestamp
Orphaned key_decisions with NULL task_id but valid run_id: must be archived when the run is archived

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Archive key_decisions before deleting/archiving parent runs
Run stays active because PB run_tasks still reference it

invariants: PB tasks must always have archived_at IS NULL after archiving PA only
Shared runs must only be archived when ALL run_tasks are archived
task_relationships and task_files must be hard-deleted (count = 0), not soft-archived
userStories.notes: Use the existing setup_two_prds() helper in archive.rs tests. Existing tests like test_clear_prd_a_leaves_prd_b_intact show the pattern. These tests will initially fail since archive_prd_data doesn't exist yet.

correctness: Tests must verify BOTH that archived records have archived_at IS NOT NULL AND that non-archived records have archived_at IS NULL
style: Follow existing archive.rs test patterns; use setup_two_prds() helper

userStories.title: Initial tests for soft-archive function and query filters

down_sql: DROP INDEX + ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN for all 4 + UPDATE global_state SET schema_version = 13

userStories.description: Create migration v14 that adds archived_at TEXT DEFAULT NULL to tasks, runs, run_tasks, and key_decisions tables. Add indexes on each. Register in migrations/mod.rs and bump CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION to 14.
userStories.notes: Follow v13.rs as template. Index naming: idx_tasks_archived_at, idx_runs_archived_at, etc. SQLite DROP COLUMN requires >= 3.35.0; rusqlite 0.31 bundles 3.45+.

correctness: All 4 tables must get the column; missing one causes runtime errors downstream
performance: Indexes are critical — filtered queries must use them

userStories.title: Migration v14: Add archived_at columns and indexes

run_tasks: UPDATE SET archived_at instead of DELETE, with AND archived_at IS NULL guard
key_decisions: UPDATE SET archived_at by task prefix, then by orphaned runs

tasks: UPDATE SET archived_at instead of DELETE, with AND archived_at IS NULL guard
task_relationships and task_files still hard-deleted
prd_files and prd_metadata still hard-deleted

consumers.mitigation: No change needed — archive flow internally calls renamed function

userStories.description: Rename clear_prd_data to archive_prd_data in src/loop_engine/archive.rs. Change DELETE to UPDATE SET archived_at = datetime('now') for: run_tasks, key_decisions, runs, tasks. Keep hard-DELETE for task_relationships, task_files, prd_files, prd_metadata. Handle shared runs (only archive when ALL run_tasks are archived). Update global_state NULL-outs and counter reset to filter archived_at IS NULL. Update caller and all existing tests.
userStories.notes: The temporary hard-delete fix for key_decisions (commit a80bb46) should be replaced by the soft-archive approach. The existing test test_clear_prd_with_key_decisions_does_not_fk_violate should be updated to verify soft-archive behavior instead.

correctness: Shared runs MUST NOT be archived if any active run_tasks still reference them
Re-archiving must be idempotent (AND archived_at IS NULL prevents double-archiving)
performance: Single transaction for all operations — no partial archive state on failure
style: Follow retired_at pattern from learnings: UPDATE SET archived_at = datetime('now') WHERE ... AND archived_at IS NULL

userStories.title: Refactor clear_prd_data to archive_prd_data with soft-delete

userStories.description: Add AND archived_at IS NULL to all listing/aggregation queries in loop_engine core modules. These are mechanical one-line additions to existing SQL queries.
userStories.notes: Read each file first to find the exact query locations. The line numbers from the PRD are approximate — verify against current code. Pattern: add AND archived_at IS NULL for direct table queries, or AND t.archived_at IS NULL for aliased JOINs. For run_tasks queries in calibrate.rs, filter on run_tasks.archived_at IS NULL.

correctness: Every aggregation/listing query must filter — missing one causes archived records to leak into active workflows
performance: Indexes on archived_at ensure filter is efficient
style: Use AND archived_at IS NULL inline, matching the retired_at IS NULL pattern in learnings queries

userStories.description: Add --include-archived optional argument (with optional usize limit) to the list and history CLI commands. When set, queries include archived records with [archived] markers in output. When not set, queries filter WHERE archived_at IS NULL. Add the archived_at query filter to list.rs and history.rs as part of this task.
userStories.notes: For clap, use #[arg(long = "include-archived", num_args = 0..=1, default_missing_value = "0")] to make the limit optional. Value 0 or None means no limit. The [archived] marker should appear in the task/run title or status display. The run_tasks and key_decisions queries within history detail view should also respect the flag.

correctness: Default behavior (no flag) MUST filter out archived records — this is the most important invariant
performance: With large archived datasets, the limit parameter prevents memory issues
style: Follow existing CLI arg patterns in commands.rs

userStories.title: --include-archived flag on list and history commands

userStories.description: Change init --force prefix-scoped path to archive run_tasks, key_decisions, and runs (preserving history) before hard-deleting tasks for clean PK reimport. Global wipe (no prefix) keeps current hard-delete behavior. Also add archived_at IS NULL filters to init/import.rs queries that check for existing tasks.
userStories.notes: The PK conflict is handled by keeping hard-delete for tasks (they need clean IDs for reimport) while soft-archiving runs/key_decisions (which don't have PK conflicts with reimported data). The drop_existing_data function's prefix branch should archive first, then delete tasks.

correctness: Must preserve run and key_decision history while allowing clean task reimport
Global wipe must NOT change — it's a full reset
style: Keep the existing function structure; just change prefix branch behavior

userStories.title: init --force: archive runs/key_decisions before reimport
